notes: Spec asserts: ready → working, state stays working across all retry cycles (no flicker to ready or waiting between attempts), final working → ready. The recipe drives a deterministic failing-then-passing bash sequence (bash 'false' then bash 'echo recovered') so the parser sees one user prompt, multiple assistant→tool_use→tool_result cycles, then a terminal stop_reason. The intermediate_working phase pins the no-flicker invariant with duration_at_least_ms 2000 — long enough to span both retry cycles given typical claude latency. If the parser ever surfaces a tool_result failure as a transition to ready or waiting, this phase fires.

text: User prompt drives ready → working (spec bullet 1)
State stays working across all retry cycles — the failing bash 'false' tool_result must NOT flicker the session to ready or waiting before the recovery cycle starts (spec bullet 2)
After the final successful attempt: working → ready (spec bullet 3)
